comparisons-and-truth-values.label: Comparisons and Truth Values
comparisons-and-truth-values.summary: Use comparison operators and understand True and False results.

boolean_logic_example.title: Implement a simple boolean logic checker

evaluate_conditions.title: Create a program to evaluate multiple conditions

comparison-operators.title: What does the '==' operator do?
comparison-operators.hint: It's used to compare two values.

help.concept: The '==' operator checks if two values are equal.
help.hint_2: It returns True if both values are the same.

options.c: Checks if one value is greater than another
options.d: Checks if one value is less than another

truthiness-in-python.title: Which of the following are considered False in Python?
truthiness-in-python.hint: Focus on the main concept being tested and eliminate choices that describe something different.

help.concept: Choose the option that matches the core idea described in the question without relying on repeated wording from the answer choices.
help.hint_1: Rule out options that describe a different role, behavior, or concept than the question is testing.
help.hint_2: Pick the option that best matches the concept, not the one that simply repeats familiar wording.

fill-in-blank-logic.title: Fill in the blank: The expression '5 > 3' evaluates to ____.
fill-in-blank-logic.prompt: Choose the best value for the missing blank in the statement.

help.concept: This task checks the key idea behind fill in the blank: the expression '5 > 3' evaluates to ____..
help.hint_1: Look for the word or phrase that best matches the lesson concept.
help.hint_2: Compare the blank with the meaning of each choice, not just the wording.

boolean-logic-example.title: Implement a simple boolean logic checker
boolean-logic-example.prompt: Write a program that checks if a number is both greater than 10 and less than 20.
boolean-logic-example.hint: Focus on the programming task being asked for, not on copying final solution text.

help.concept: Build the solution from the behavior the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which steps, functions, or statements are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the solution based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

evaluate-conditions.title: Create a program to evaluate multiple conditions
evaluate-conditions.prompt: Write a program that checks if a number is even and greater than 10.
evaluate-conditions.hint: Use the modulus operator to check for evenness.

help.concept: You can use 'and' to combine checks for evenness and size.
help.hint_1: Check if the number is even using num % 2.
help.hint_2: Combine both conditions in your if statement.

intro-to-comparisons.title: Introduction to Comparisons
intro-to-comparisons.bodyMarkdown: In Python, comparison operators are used to compare two values. The result of a comparison is a boolean value: either True or False.

truth-values.title: Understanding Truth Values
truth-values.bodyMarkdown: Truth values in Python can be derived from various expressions. Knowing how to evaluate these expressions is crucial for controlling program flow.
